summary: Superimposed pre-eclampsia shows varied presentations; standard indicators may not always be evident. A significant decrease in platelets indicates strong systemic inflammation associated with late-stage preeclampsia rather than just chronic high blood pressure. Therefore, an abnormal platelet count stands out more reliably for diagnosis among given options.
